originalTitle: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
title: Big News! President Biya Gets a Helper in Cameroon!
content:
Hello, Super Star Listeners!

Hello, my amazing friends! It's me, your kid-friendly news reporter from wonderful Africa! I have some super exciting and interesting news to share with you today. We are going to fly over to a beautiful country in Africa called Cameroon. Can you say Cameroon? It sounds like "Cam-uh-roon." It's a land full of amazing nature and friendly people. Get ready for a special story about a big change!

Meet President Biya

In Cameroon, there is a very important leader. He is called the President. His name is President Paul Biya. He is a very, very old man. He is 93 years old! Wow! That's older than your grandparents, and maybe even your great-grandparents! Imagine having 93 birthday cakes! He has been the President of Cameroon for a super long time. Can you guess how long? He has been President for 43 years! That's more birthdays than most grown-ups have had! He has been working hard for his country for a very, very long time. **Did you know?** Being a President is a bit like being the captain of a very big ship. The President helps guide the whole country and makes sure things run smoothly and safely for everyone who lives there.

What Does a President Do?

A President has a very big job. They help make important decisions for their country every single day. They work to make sure children can go to school and learn new things. They help make sure there are good roads for cars and buses. They also help make sure people have jobs so they can earn money. It is a lot of work! It's like being the head of a very, very big family, looking after everyone in the country. President Biya has been doing this big job all by himself for many, many years. **Did you know?** Cameroon is a country in West Africa. It has sunny beaches where the ocean meets the land, tall mountains, and even thick rainforests! Many different animals live there, like elephants and monkeys, swinging through the trees.

Getting a Special Helper: A Deputy!

Now, here is the big, big news! After all these years, President Biya is going to get a special helper. This helper is called a "deputy." Can you say "dep-yoo-tee"? A deputy is someone who works very closely with the main leader. They help with important tasks and decisions, just like a helper bee in a hive. They are like a co-captain on the big ship of the country. For the very first time in 43 years, President Biya will have a deputy by his side! This is a brand new change! **Did you know?** Having a helper means you can share the work! If you are building a big sandcastle, it's always easier and more fun when a friend helps you scoop sand and build towers, right? Two sets of hands are better than one!

Why Is This Big News?

Imagine doing your chores all by yourself every single day for 43 years! That's a super long time to do everything alone. That's a little bit like what President Biya has been doing for his country. So, getting a deputy is a really big deal because it means he will finally have someone important to help him. It's a new chapter for Cameroon and its President. It shows that even the most important leaders sometimes need a helping hand and a friend to work with. This is a very exciting and happy change for the country! **Did you know?** Cameroon is sometimes called "Africa in miniature" because it has so many different types of beautiful landscapes and amazing cultures, just like the whole continent of Africa in one country!

What Does a Deputy Do?

The deputy will work very closely with President Biya. They will help listen to people from all over the country, help make plans for the future, and ensure important things get done. If the President is busy with one very big thing, the deputy can help with another big thing. It means there will be two pairs of eyes and two brains working together for the good of all the people in Cameroon. Teamwork makes the dream work, right? Having a deputy means the President can get even more done and make even better decisions for everyone. **Did you know?** Many countries around the world have leaders who work with deputies or vice presidents. It's a very common and smart way to share big responsibilities and make sure everything runs smoothly!

Teamwork Makes Everything Better!

This news reminds us how important teamwork is in life. When you play a game with your friends, you work together as a team to win. When you help your family with chores at home, you work as a team to make your house clean. The same is true for running a country! With a deputy, President Biya will have someone to talk to, share clever ideas with, and divide up all the big, important jobs. This can make the country run even smoother and better for every child, every grown-up, and every family who lives there. Everyone wins with teamwork! **Did you know?** There are over 27 million people who live in Cameroon! That's a lot of people for one President to look after and make decisions for! Having a deputy will definitely help make sure everyone is heard and cared for.

A New Chapter for Cameroon

So, this is a very special moment for Cameroon. A very experienced leader, President Biya, is welcoming a new partner to help him guide the country. It shows that even after a very, very long time, things can change for the better. We wish President Biya and his new deputy all the best as they work together to lead the wonderful and amazing people of Cameroon. It’s an exciting time to see this new kind of teamwork begin, bringing new energy and help to the country! Hooray for teamwork! **Did you know?** Some of the people in Cameroon speak French, and others speak English! It's a country with many different languages and cultures living together peacefully and happily.
summary: President Paul Biya of Cameroon, who is 93 years old and has been President for 43 years, is getting a special helper called a deputy for the very first time.
whyMatters: This is important because it means the very old President will now have someone to share his big job with, helping to guide the country of Cameroon better with teamwork, making sure more gets done for everyone.

originalTitle: Senegal bans ministers from foreign travel as oil price rise bites
title: Senegal's Big Plan: Saving Money for Everyone!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! I'm your super kid-friendly reporter from Africa, ready to share some cool news with you. Today, we're zooming over to a beautiful country called Senegal! It's right here in West Africa, full of sunshine and friendly faces. Senegal has a very smart plan to help its people, and it's all about saving money. Let's find out more!

Meet Senegal and Its Leaders!

Senegal is a wonderful country with many towns and cities. Its capital city is Dakar, which is by the big blue ocean. Just like your school has a principal or your town has a mayor, a country has leaders. These leaders are called 'ministers'. Ministers help run the country. They make important decisions about things like schools, hospitals, and roads. They work very hard to make sure everyone in Senegal is happy and safe. They want the best for all the children and families.

No More Flying for Ministers?

Now, here's the big news! The President of Senegal has asked all the ministers not to travel to other countries for a little while. This means no more flying in airplanes to visit faraway places. It's like your parents saying, 'Let's not go on a long trip this month, let's save that money instead!' This is a very important step for Senegal. It's a big change for the ministers who sometimes need to meet leaders from other countries. But they understand why this is important for their own country. They are helping Senegal save money.

Did You Know?

Did you know? Senegal is famous for its lively music and delicious food! One very popular and tasty dish is called 'Thieboudienne' (pronounced Cheb-oo-jen), which is made with fish, rice, and vegetables! It's super yummy and a favorite for many families!

Why Are We Saving Money? It's About Fuel!

You might be wondering, 'Why are they doing this?' Well, it's all about something called 'fuel'. What is fuel? Fuel is what cars, buses, and trucks need to move. It's like the food your body needs to run and play! Without fuel, these vehicles can't go anywhere. Fuel helps people get to school every day. It helps farmers take their fresh food to the market to sell. It also helps doctors visit sick people in faraway villages. Without fuel, things would slow down a lot in Senegal, and it would be hard for people to do what they need to do.

Where Does Fuel Come From?

Most of the fuel we use, like gasoline or diesel for cars, comes from something special called 'oil'. Oil is a black, thick, sticky liquid found deep, deep underground. It takes a lot of hard work and big machines to get the oil out of the earth and then turn it into the fuel we use every day. When the price of oil goes up, the price of fuel goes up too. And guess what? The price of oil has gone up a lot recently! This means it costs much more money for Senegal to buy all the fuel it needs for its country.

Oil Prices are Going Up!

Imagine you go to the shop to buy your favorite candy. One day, it costs 1 coin. The next day, it costs 2 coins! That's a bit like what's happening with oil. The cost of oil is now almost double what the government of Senegal thought it would be. This means it costs twice as much to buy the fuel needed for all the cars, buses, and machines in the country. That's a huge extra cost! It's like needing to spend double your pocket money just for your snacks.

Did You Know?

Did you know? Oil was formed from tiny plants and animals that lived in the oceans millions and millions of years ago! Over time, they got buried under layers of rock and dirt, and slowly turned into oil. It's like nature's very old, very special juice that gives us energy!

Senegal's Money is Precious!

Just like your family has a budget for how much money they can spend each month, the government of Senegal also has a budget. They plan how much money they will spend on different important things each year. This includes building new schools, buying medicines for hospitals, and fixing roads so everyone can travel safely. But with fuel costing so much more money, it's like a big hole has appeared in their money jar! They have less money left for other important things if they spend so much on fuel. This makes it hard to pay for everything they planned.

Saving Money Helps Everyone

So, by asking ministers not to travel to other countries, Senegal is saving a lot of money! When ministers travel, they need expensive airplane tickets, places to stay (hotels), and other things. These costs can add up very quickly. By staying in Senegal, they are saving those precious coins for the country. It's a smart way to be careful with money, especially when things cost more than expected. Every bit of money saved makes a difference! The ministers are showing they care about the country's finances.

What Happens With the Saved Money?

This saved money can then be used for important things right here in Senegal. Maybe it can help build a new classroom for children who need more space to learn. Or buy more medicines and bandages for people who are not feeling well in hospitals. Or even help fix the roads so buses and cars can travel more easily and safely! It's like finding extra money to buy something really important for your family, instead of spending it on something less urgent. This money will help make life better for many people in Senegal, especially children.

Did You Know?

Did you know? When you save money, even a little bit from your pocket money, it can grow bigger over time! Saving helps you reach your goals, just like Senegal is saving its money to reach its goals for its people and make their country stronger!

Everyone is Helping!

This smart plan shows us that everyone, even important leaders, can help save money when times are a bit tough. It's about working together as a team. When everyone helps and makes smart choices, the country can stay strong and keep growing. The leaders of Senegal are setting a great example for all of us. They are showing that putting the needs of the country first is what really matters. They are making sure the country's money is used wisely for the most important things.

A Bright Future for Senegal!

Senegal is a country that cares deeply about its people. By making smart choices like this, they are making sure that their money goes to the most important things. It means more help for schools, more help for hospitals, and a better life for all the children and families in Senegal. So, even though it's a big decision, it's a kind and smart one. Hooray for Senegal's smart saving plan! They are working hard to build a bright and happy future for everyone. And that's fantastic news!
summary: Senegal's leaders are choosing not to fly to other countries right now to save money because the cost of fuel, like for cars and buses, has become much more expensive.
whyMatters: This matters because by saving money on travel, Senegal can use that money for important things like schools, hospitals, and roads, helping all its people live better and making the country stronger.

originalTitle: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
title: Abuja's Big Story: Friends Who Need Our Ears!
content:
Hello from Sunny Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter, ready to share a very important story from our big, beautiful continent, Africa! Today, we're going to fly our imaginary plane to a country called Nigeria. It's a land of amazing music, delicious food, and wonderful people. And in Nigeria, there's a special city called Abuja. It's the capital city, and we have a story about some friends there who need us to listen with our hearts.

What is a Capital City?

First, let's talk about what a capital city is. Imagine your house. Your parents or guardians might make important decisions there, right? A capital city is kind of like the main house for a whole country! It's where the President lives and works. It's where big decisions are made that help everyone in the country. It's a very important place! Did you know? Nigeria is one of the biggest countries in Africa! It has so many people, like a giant school full of friends! Many years ago, Nigeria needed a brand-new capital city. The old one was getting too crowded. So, they chose a special spot and decided to build a beautiful new city called Abuja. They wanted it to be shiny, modern, and a great place for everyone.

Moving Day Blues for Our Friends

But guess what? Before Abuja was built, there were already people living in that special spot. They had their homes there, their farms, their schools, and their playgrounds. It was their land, where their families had lived for a very, very long time. To build the new capital city, these people had to move. This is sometimes called being 'evicted.' It means they had to leave their homes so the new buildings could go up. Imagine someone telling you that you have to move out of your cozy bedroom so they can build a big, new library there. It would feel strange and maybe a little sad, right? Did you know? Building a whole new city takes many, many years! It's a huge project, like building a giant Lego castle, but much bigger and with real houses and roads!

Promises, Promises!

When our friends had to move, the grown-ups who were building the city made promises to them. A promise is when you say you will definitely do something. Like when your parent promises you a story before bed, or a friend promises to share their toys. What were the promises? They told our friends, "Don't worry! We will give you new homes, new schools for your children, and new places to work. Your life will be even better in a new place!" These promises made our friends feel a little bit better about leaving their old homes. They trusted the grown-ups. They hoped for new, safe places to live. They hoped for good schools for their kids to learn and play. They hoped for jobs so they could earn money and buy food and clothes for their families. These were really important promises.

Sad Hearts and Unkept Promises

But here's the sad part of our story. Many years have passed since our friends moved from their homes to make way for Abuja. And for many of them, the promises were not kept. Imagine if someone promised you a big, yummy ice cream cone, but then they never gave it to you. You would feel disappointed, wouldn't you? Our friends feel even more disappointed, and also a bit forgotten. Some of them still don't have good homes. Some of their children don't have nice schools to go to. They feel like their voices haven't been heard. They feel like the grown-ups forgot about them after the city was built. They are still waiting for the new, better life that was promised. Did you know? When people feel forgotten, it's like they're shouting, but no one is listening. It can make them feel very sad and alone.

What Our Friends Want Now

So, what do our friends want? They are saying, "We want a voice in our land!" This means they want the grown-ups in the government to listen to them. They want to talk about the promises that were made and make sure they are finally kept. They want safe homes, just like you have. They want schools where their children can learn and grow. They want to be treated fairly and with kindness. They want to feel like they are part of Nigeria, just like everyone else. They just want the promises to come true.

Let's All Be Kind and Listen

It's very important for everyone, especially those in charge, to listen to people and keep their promises. When we listen to each other, we can solve problems and make sure everyone feels happy and safe. This story from Abuja reminds us that every person's home and happiness are very important. Let's hope that soon, all our friends in Abuja will have their promises fulfilled and live happily in our amazing Nigeria! Thank you for listening, little reporters! See you next time!
summary: People who moved from their homes to build Nigeria's capital, Abuja, say the government did not keep its promises of new homes and schools, and they want their voices to be heard.
whyMatters: This story is important because everyone deserves a safe home and for promises to be kept, and it reminds us to listen to people and treat them fairly, especially when big changes happen.
